content: Identifying market structure is retrospective; what looks like a clear HH/HL pattern in hindsight is ambiguous in real-time. Structure breaks are frequent and often false, generating whipsaws. The definition of 'significant' highs/lows is subjective. In choppy markets, structure analysis produces constant false signals. Efficient market theorists argue structure is simply random walk with drift. Algo trading deliberately creates false structure breaks to trigger retail stops before reversing.
